---
name: CSIRS 128T128R Project Overview
description: 5G Advanced PHY simulation project - CSI-RS and CSI Feedback from Rel-15/16 to Rel-19 for 128T128R massive MIMO
type: project
---

Main MATLAB file: `CSIRS_128T128R_v1_0.m` in the project root.
Author: ThangTQ23 - VSI, Date: 2026-03

**System Configuration:**
- Carrier: 52 PRBs, SCS 30 kHz (10 MHz), n78 (3.5 GHz)
- Antenna: 128T (gNB: 4V×16H×2pol) x 4R (UE: 1V×2H×2pol)
- CSI-RS: 4 NZP-CSI-RS Resources × 32 ports (Row 18, CDM8 = FD-CDM2 × TD-CDM4)
- Layout: 2-slot (Slot 0: R0 l0=2, R1 l0=8; Slot 1: R2 l0=2, R3 l0=8)
- Channel: CDL-B (changed from CDL-C for rank-4 test), DS=100ns, Doppler=5Hz, SNR=20dB
- Custom library paths: `5g/` and `wirelessnetwork/`

**4 Approaches (selectedApproach = 'ALL'):**
- A: Rel-15/16 Per-32-Port Type I Single Panel (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1), struct-based reportConfig, PanelDimensions=[8,2]
- B: Full 128-port SVD upper bound (ideal reference), uses svd(H_wb,'econ')
- C: Rel-19 Type I Single-Panel Mode A (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a), CodebookType='typeI-SinglePanel-r19', CodebookMode=1, PanelDimensions=[1,16,4]
- D: Rel-19 Type I Single-Panel Mode B (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a), same config as C but CodebookMode=2

**Pipeline (12 sections):**
1. System Config → 2. CSI-RS Resource Config → 3. Signal Gen & Grid Map →
4. Grid Visualization → 5. CDL Channel Model → 6. TX through Channel + AWGN →
7. Timing Sync + OFDM Demod → 8. Channel Estimation (per resource) →
9. CE Error Analysis (NMSE) → 10. CSI Feedback (PMI/RI/CQI for A/B/C/D) →
11. Beamforming Pattern Visualization → 12. Summary Table

**Key internal functions used:**
- `nr5g.internal.nrRISelect`, `nr5g.internal.nrCQISelect` (for Approach A)
- `nr5g.internal.nrPMIReport` (for Approach C & D)
- `nrChannelEstimate` with CDMLengths=[2,4] (Row 18 CDM8)

**Why:** Goal is to evaluate gain of Rel-19 Refined Type I codebook over Rel-15/16 for massive MIMO 128T128R. Known limitation: ModeA TypeIRel19 gives RI=1 with CDL-C; CDL-B was introduced to test higher rank.

**Current terminal output state (as of 2026-03-30): user is satisfied.**
Key output fixes applied in this session:
- Approach A: added capacity column (SVD per 32-port sub-block, select max → R2 = 18.18 bits/s/Hz)
- Approach B: replaced single `Singular values` line with per-SV table (SV#, Singular val, SNR dB, Cap bits/s/Hz)
- Approach C/D: changed `bpcu` → `bits/s/Hz`, added header "RI selection (capacity per rank candidate):"
- Comparison table: removed duplicate SVD block, added Approach A row, all 4 approaches now comparable
- Summary block: all approaches now show RI / Cap / CQI in consistent format

**How to apply:** When suggesting changes, keep the 12-section pipeline structure. Use `nr5g.internal.*` namespace for internal PMI/RI/CQI functions. Approach B (SVD) is always the upper bound reference for capacity comparison. Do NOT revert terminal output formatting — user has approved current state.

4	%  Configuration:

6	%    - 2-slot simulation: R0+R1 in Slot 0, R2+R3 in Slot 1
7	%    - Channel: CDL-C (spatial, suitable for massive MIMO)
8	%    - Pipeline: Signal Gen -> Channel -> CE -> CSI Feedback (PMI/RI/CQI)
9	%
10	%  References:
11	%    - TS 38.211 S7.4.1.5 (CSI-RS signal generation)
12	%    - TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1  (Type I Single Panel, Rel-15/16)

20	%rng(100);  % Reproducible

29	
30	%% NR Cell Performance with Downlink MU-MIMO

62	    nResources, nPortsPerRes, nTxAntennas);
63

66	%  ========================================================================
67	%
68	%  Row 18: 32 ports, CDM8 (FD-CDM2 x TD-CDM4), density 0.5
69	%    - k' = 0,1       (2 subcarriers per CDM group, FD-CDM2)
70	%    - l' = 0,1,2,3   (4 symbols per CDM group, TD-CDM4)
71	%    - 8 CDM groups x 4 ports/group = 32 ports

73	%
74	%  4 resources x 4 symbols/resource = 16 symbols > 14 symbols/slot
75	%  -> must spread across 2 consecutive slots

77	%  2-slot layout (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a, Rel-19):
78	%    Slot 0: R0 (l0=2, ports 0-31)   -> symbols 2,3,4,5

%% 128T128R NZP-CSI-RS Channel Estimation & CSI Feedback
%  Project: CSI-RS experiment for Massive MIMO 128T128R
%
%  Configuration:
%    - 128 CSI-RS ports = 4 NZP-CSI-RS Resources x 32 ports (Row 18, CDM8)
%    - 2-slot simulation: R0+R1 in Slot 0, R2+R3 in Slot 1
%    - Channel: CDL-C (spatial, suitable for massive MIMO)
%    - Pipeline: Signal Gen -> Channel -> CE -> CSI Feedback (PMI/RI/CQI)
%
%  References:
%    - TS 38.211 S7.4.1.5 (CSI-RS signal generation)
%    - TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1  (Type I Single Panel, Rel-15/16)
%    - TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a (Refined Type I Single Panel, Rel-19)
%    - TS 38.331 (NZP-CSI-RS-ResourceSet)
%
%  Author: ThangTQ23 - VSI
%  Date:   2026-03

clear; close all; clc;
%rng(100);  % Reproducible

%% Approach Selection
%  'A'   - Rel-15/16 per-32-port Type I Single Panel (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1)
%  'B'   - Full 128-port SVD upper bound (ideal reference)
%  'C'   - Rel-19 Type I Single-Panel Mode A (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a)
%  'D'   - Rel-19 Type I Single-Panel Mode B (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a)
%  'ALL' - Run all approaches and print comparison table
selectedApproach = 'ALL';

%% NR Cell Performance with Downlink MU-MIMO
% Custom Path Library
custom_lib_path_5g = fullfile(pwd, '5g');
custom_lib_path_wn = fullfile(pwd, 'wirelessnetwork');

addpath(genpath(custom_lib_path_5g));
addpath(genpath(custom_lib_path_wn));

rehash toolboxcache;

%% ========================================================================
%  SECTION 1: SYSTEM CONFIGURATION
%  ========================================================================

% --- Carrier ---
carrier = nrCarrierConfig;
carrier.NSizeGrid           = 52;          % 10 MHz @ SCS 30 kHz
carrier.SubcarrierSpacing   = 30;          % kHz
carrier.NSlot               = 0;
carrier.NFrame              = 0;
carrier.CyclicPrefix        = 'normal';

% --- Antenna Configuration ---
nTxAntennas  = 128;                        % gNB antennas
nRxAntennas  = 4;                          % UE antennas (typical)
nPortsPerRes = 32;                         % Ports per CSI-RS resource
nResources   = nTxAntennas / nPortsPerRes; % = 4 resources

fprintf('=== 128T128R CSI-RS Project ===\n');
fprintf('Carrier: %d PRBs, SCS %d kHz\n', carrier.NSizeGrid, carrier.SubcarrierSpacing);
fprintf('Antenna: %dT%dR\n', nTxAntennas, nRxAntennas);
fprintf('CSI-RS: %d resources x %d ports = %d total ports\n', ...
    nResources, nPortsPerRes, nTxAntennas);

%% ========================================================================
%  SECTION 2: CONFIGURE 4 NZP-CSI-RS RESOURCES (Row 18, CDM8)
%  ========================================================================
%
%  Row 18: 32 ports, CDM8 (FD-CDM2 x TD-CDM4), density 0.5
%    - k' = 0,1       (2 subcarriers per CDM group, FD-CDM2)
%    - l' = 0,1,2,3   (4 symbols per CDM group, TD-CDM4)
%    - 8 CDM groups x 4 ports/group = 32 ports
%    - SymbolLocations: single scalar l0; toolbox auto-expands to l0,l0+1,l0+2,l0+3
%
%  4 resources x 4 symbols/resource = 16 symbols > 14 symbols/slot
%  -> must spread across 2 consecutive slots
%
%  2-slot layout (TS 38.214 S5.2.2.2.1a, Rel-19):
%    Slot 0: R0 (l0=2, ports 0-31)   -> symbols 2,3,4,5
%            R1 (l0=8, ports 32-63)  -> symbols 8,9,10,11
%    Slot 1: R2 (l0=2, ports 64-95)  -> symbols 2,3,4,5

content.thinking:
Now I have good context. Let me analyze what the customer is requesting.

The customer wants:
1. "Xem cả mode A và mode B" - View both Mode A and Mode B (currently these are Approach C and D in the simulation)
2. "Kết quả các phương án trình bày dưới dạng RI trung bình, MCS trung bình, throughput trung bình" - Present results as average RI, average MCS, average throughput
3. "Tại 3-4 mức bán kính (SNR)" - At 3-4 distance/radius levels (SNR levels), representing near, medium, far


4. "Có 1 phương án tốt nhất làm tham chiếu" - Have 1 best approach as reference (clearly Approach B - SVD upper bound)
5. "Xem scheme nào đó đạt được bao nhiêu % so với scheme lý tưởng" - See what % each scheme achieves vs the ideal reference

So the simulation currently runs at a single SNR point, but I need to sweep across multiple SNR values representing different distances, calculate average metrics at each level, and build a comparison table showing how each approach performs relative to the ideal reference.

The main changes are: expanding from a fixed SNR=20dB to 3-4 SNR points (like -5, 5, 15, 25 dB), adding throughput as a metric alongside RI and CQI/MCS, and computing averages either through Monte Carlo simulations across channel realizations or by averaging across subcarriers and time.

Now I need to check the current code structure around SNR configuration and the output formatting to understand how to integrate these changes.

content.text:
## Phân tích yêu cầu khách hàng

Đây là **3 thay đổi lớn** so với code hiện tại:

### 1. Thêm chiều SNR sweep (hiện tại: single point)
Code hiện tại chạy tại 1 điểm SNR cố định. Khách muốn chạy tại **3–4 mức SNR**, tương ứng với khoảng cách UE:
- Gần (high SNR, e.g. ~25 dB)
- Trung bình (e.g. ~10 dB)
- Xa (low SNR, e.g. ~0 dB hoặc −5 dB)

→ Cần vòng lặp `for snr = snrList`, chạy toàn bộ pipeline (channel → CE → CSI feedback) tại mỗi điểm.

### 2. Thêm metric "throughput trung bình" + đổi output format
Hiện tại output là snapshot 1 lần chạy. Khách muốn **trung bình** (cần nhiều channel realizations / Monte Carlo) của:
- **RI trung bình** — đã có, cần average over runs
- **MCS trung bình** — CQI → MCS mapping (TS 38.214 Table 5.2.2.1-3)
- **Throughput trung bình** — cần tính bps: `f(MCS, RI, BW, layers, overhead)`

### 3. Thêm cột % so với scheme tham chiếu
- **Scheme tham chiếu = Approach B (SVD upper bound)** — đây là "ideal" đã có sẵn
- Mỗi approach hiển thị thêm `X% so với B`

---

## Tóm tắt công việc cần làm

| Hạng mục | Nội dung |
|---|---|
| **SNR sweep** | Thêm loop 3–4 điểm SNR, chạy lại pipeline per point |
| **Monte Carlo** | Nhiều channel realizations per SNR để lấy trung bình |
| **MCS mapping** | CQI → MCS theo bảng 3GPP |
| **Throughput** | Tính từ MCS + RI + BW (e.g. 52 PRB × 12 SC) |
| **Output table** | Bảng 2D: rows = approaches, cols = SNR levels, + cột % vs B |

---

Bạn muốn tôi bắt đầu implement phần nào trước? Thường hợp lý nhất là: **SNR sweep loop → averaging loop → throughput calc → output table**.

Tạm dừng lại 1 chút, hãy nói tới điều kiện mô phỏng, trước đây khách từng gửi cho tôi điều kiện mô phỏng, tôi không nói sẽ apply toàn bộ vào code hiện tại, những trước hết hãy phân tích điều kiện khách muốn là gì? chúng ta có thể thay đổi gì? Anh check Matlab và em theo đặc tính:

 

% ====================== gNB PARAMETERS ======================

gNBConfig = struct();

gNBConfig.Position = [0 0 0];          % Vị trí gNB [x,y,z] (m)

gNBConfig.TransmitPower = 70;          % Công suất phát (dBm)

 

ghi chú:

Hệ thóng hoạt động:

-   Công suất mỗi port: 2.5W.

-   Công suất mỗi port theo dBm: 34dBm.

-   Số lượng kênh phát: 128T.

-   Tổng công suất: 320W

-   Tổng công suất(dBm) không tính gain antenna: 55dBm.

-   Tổng công suất(dBm) tính gain antenna: 76dBm.

-   Vì vậy: tạm lấy trung bình cho mô phỏng khoảng 70dBm.

 

gNBConfig.SubcarrierSpacing = 30000;   % Subcarrier spacing (Hz)

gNBConfig.CarrierFrequency = 4.9e9;    % Tần số sóng mang (Hz) – Tần số lựa chọn 4.9Ghz

gNBConfig.ChannelBandwidth = 100e6;      % Băng thông kênh (Hz)

gNBConfig.NumTransmitAntennas = 128;    % Số anten phát gNB

gNBConfig.NumReceiveAntennas = 128;     % Số anten thu gNB – Hệ thống hoạt động là 128TRx

gNBConfig.ReceiveGain = 32;            % Gain thu (dBi)  - Lưu ý đây là đơn vị dBi

gNBConfig.DuplexMode = "TDD";          % Chế độ song công: "TDD" hoặc "FDD"

gNBConfig.SRSPeriodicity = 5;         % Chu kỳ truyền SRS (slots) – Nếu tính toán với tốc độ tối đa cho người di chuyển là 30km/h, ở tần số trạm là 4.9Ghz thì thời gian duy trì ổn định của kênh truyền phải là Tc ~ 9/(16*pi*fd) = 1/(4*136.1) = 1.84ms tương đương 3.68 Slot, Vì vậy để đảm bảo cho kênh truyền thay đổi nhanh và duy trì tính chính xác của CSI thì UE phải report CSI index hoặc SRS phải nhỏ hơn 3.68 Slot hoặc lấy trung bình là 4 Slot. Tuy nhiên khung hệ thống đang dùng là 7D1S2U hoặc 3D1S1U, chỉ được report là sau 5 Slot hoặc 10 Slot; Vậy lấy giá trị là 5.

 

% ====================== UE PARAMETERS ======================

ueConfig = struct();

ueConfig.NumUEs = 512;                   % Số lượng UE – Số lượng UE 512

ueConfig.NumReceiveAntennas = 4;        % Số anten thu mỗi UE – Số lượng antenna của UE mặc định là 4Rx2Tx

ueConfig.ReceiveGain = 0;               % Gain thu UE (dBi) – Độ lợi antenna của thiết bị thương mai giao động từ -3dBi đến 3 dBi ; test lấy tiêu chuẩn 0dBi nhưng thêm case cho trường hợp +/-1dBi

ueConfig.DistanceFromgNB = 1200;         % Khoảng cách từ UE đến gNB (m) – Bán kính cell tối đa 1200m

ueConfig.AzimuthRange = [-30 30];       % Dải góc phương vị (degrees) – Góc phương vị chỉ tập trung vào +/- 30

ueConfig.ElevationAngle = 0;            % Góc nâng (degrees).

 

% ====================== MU-MIMO CONFIGURATION ======================

muMIMOConfig = struct();

muMIMOConfig.MaxNumUsersPaired = 12;     % Số UE tối đa ghép chung – UE ghép chung 12.

muMIMOConfig.MinNumRBs = 3;             % Số RB tối thiểu cho mỗi UE – Giá trị tối thiểu RB đang dùng trong hệ thống là 3RB

muMIMOConfig.SemiOrthogonalityFactor = 0.9; % Độ trực giao tối thiểu

CQI           Modulation   SINR

0            N/A          <-7db

15           256 QAM      >25dB

schedulerConfig.MaxNumUsersPerTTI = 64;     % Số UE tối đa mỗi TTI – Số UE/TTI tối đa là 66UE/TTI với PDSCH; 64 UE/TTI với PUSCH; 320 PUCCH; lấy trung bình 64UE/TTI

schedulerConfig.MUMIMOConfigDL = muMIMOConfig; % Cấu hình MU-MIMO

 

% ====================== CHANNEL MODEL PARAMETERS ======================

channelConfig = struct();

channelConfig.DelayProfile = "CDL-D";       % Delay profile

channelConfig.DelaySpread = 450e-9;         % Delay spread (s) – Xem xét ở môi trường Urban Macro 300-500ns; Lấy giá trị trung bình 450ns

channelConfig.MaxDopplerShift = 136;          % Doppler shift (Hz) - 0 cho static – Dự kiến với tốc độ người dùng không quá 30kkm/h ~ 8.33m/s thì Doppler Shift tính khoảng: fd = (8.33*4.9X10^9)/3*10^8 = 136.1 Hz.

channelConfig.TransmitArrayOrientation = [60 0 0]'; % Hướng anten phát [azimuth, downtilt, roll] – Không rõ đây là đơn vị gì, nếu là azimuth, downtitl, roll của trạm phải là azimuth = từ -30 đến 30 ( 60 độ), downtitle = 0 và roll =0 theo hệ số phương vị và góc nâng của trạm đã tính ở trên.

channelConfig.ChannelFiltering = false;     % Bật/tắt channel filtering – đây là tham số gì làm rõ cho anh.

## Phân tích điều kiện mô phỏng khách hàng

### So sánh với code hiện tại

| Tham số | **Code hiện tại** | **Khách yêu cầu** | Có thể thay đổi? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Carrier freq | 3.5 GHz (n78) | **4.9 GHz** | ✅ Dễ |
| Bandwidth | 10 MHz (52 PRBs) | **100 MHz (273 PRBs)** | ⚠️ Xem phân tích |
| SCS | 30 kHz | 30 kHz | Giữ nguyên |
| TX power | N/A (normalized) | 70 dBm | ✅ Dùng cho SNR sweep |
| Channel model | CDL-B | **CDL-D** | ❌ Xung đột mục tiêu |
| Delay spread | 100 ns | **450 ns** | ✅ Dễ |
| Doppler | 5 Hz | **136 Hz** (30 km/h @ 4.9 GHz) | ✅ Nhất quán, dễ |
| gNB antennas | 128T | 128T | Giữ nguyên |
| UE antennas | 4R | 4R | Giữ nguyên |
| NumUEs / Scheduler | N/A | 512 UE, MU-MIMO | ❌ Không áp dụng |

---

### Phân tích từng nhóm tham số

#### Nhóm 1: Áp dụng ngay được ✅

```
channel.CarrierFrequency = 4.9e9;    % 3.5→4.9 GHz
channel.DelaySpread      = 450e-9;   % 100→450 ns (Urban Macro)
channel.MaximumDopplerShift = 136;   % 5→136 Hz (30 km/h @ 4.9 GHz)
```

Lý do: Nhất quán với nhau, khách đã tính đúng (fd = v×fc/c = 8.33×4.9e9/3e8 = 136 Hz), phản ánh môi trường Urban Macro thực tế hơn.

---

#### Nhóm 2: Cần thảo luận ⚠️ — Bandwidth 100 MHz

Hiện tại `NSizeGrid = 52` → **10 MHz**. Khách muốn 100 MHz → `NSizeGrid = 273`.

Ảnh hưởng:
- Grid size tăng ~5×: `[624×14×128]` → `[3276×14×128]` — nặng hơn nhiều
- Channel estimation matrix tăng tương ứng
- PMI/RI computation sẽ nặng hơn đáng kể

> **Recommendation:** Có thể áp dụng, nhưng nên **test trước với 52 PRBs**, sau khi SNR sweep hoạt động đúng mới nâng lên 273 PRBs.

---

#### Nhóm 3: Xung đột với mục tiêu ❌ — CDL-D

Đây là điểm quan trọng nhất:

| Channel | Đặc điểm | RI outcome |
|---|---|---|
| CDL-B | Near-uniform SV → [12,11,10,9] dB | RI=4, test được Mode A/B |
| CDL-C | Urban Macro NLOS, SV1 dominant | RI=1 (đã xác nhận trong memory) |
| **CDL-D** | **LOS dominant, K-factor lớn** | **RI=1 (worse than CDL-C)** |

Nếu chạy CDL-D sẽ gần như chắc chắn bị RI=1 cho cả 4 approach → **không phân biệt được Mode A vs Mode B**, mất ý nghĩa so sánh.

> **Recommendation:** Giải thích cho khách: CDL-D là LOS (thường ở gần trạm, bán kính nhỏ), không phù hợp test rank diversity. Đề xuất dùng **CDL-B hoặc CDL-C cho mức trung bình/xa**, CDL-D chỉ dùng nếu muốn test trường hợp gần trạm (rank-1 expected).

---

#### Nhóm 4: Không áp dụng được ❌ — MU-MIMO/Scheduler/512 UE

Các tham số `muMIMOConfig`, `schedulerConfig`, `ueConfig.NumUEs = 512` là **system-level simulation**. Code hiện tại là **link-level** (1 UE, đánh giá chất lượng CSI feedback). Đây là 2 tầng khác nhau:

```
System-level:  512 UE → Scheduler → MU-MIMO pairing → Throughput aggregate
Link-level:    1 UE → Channel → CE → PMI/RI/CQI feedback ← code hiện tại
```

Khách muốn **throughput trung bình** nên có thể ý định dùng kết quả link-level để ước tính throughput per-UE, không cần simulate đủ 512 UE.

---

- Doppler: 5 → 136 Hz

```matlab
% Per UE throughput (simplified link-level)
nPRB        = carrier.NSizeGrid;        % 52
```

2. Configure 4 NZP-CSI-RS resources
3. Generate CSI-RS symbols & map to 2-slot grid
4. Visualize CSI-RS resource grid (Section 4 - can be skipped in sweep mode)
5. CDL channel model
6. Transmit through channel + AWGN
7. Timing sync + OFDM demod


8. Channel estimation (per resource, then combine)
9. CE error analysis (Section 9 - optional in sweep)
10. CSI Feedback (PMI/RI/CQI for all approaches)
11. Beamforming pattern visualization (Section 11 - can be skipped in sweep)
12. Summary

For v2, I'll organize this into a main file that handles system config and loops through SNR and Monte Carlo iterations, calling modular functions for CSI-RS configuration, channel setup, signal generation, and analysis steps that can be toggled on or off depending on whether we're running a full simulation or a sweep.

The function files will handle specific tasks: one builds the CSI-RS grid and resources, another sets up the CDL channel model with the specified antenna arrays, and additional functions will manage channel estimation, CSI feedback across all four approaches, and optional analysis sections. Now I'm mapping out the remaining helper functions—channel estimation output feeds into feedback calculations, which then determine the CQI-to-MCS conversion and throughput metrics, all wrapped up in a comparison table at the end.

Setting up the main SNR sweep loop where I initialize the channel for each realization, transmit through it, and accumulate metrics across multiple SNR points... I'm thinking through the optimization of the loop structure—the transmit grid and carrier configuration can stay outside since they don't change across realizations, but the channel object needs to be released and reset for each Monte Carlo iteration to get fresh random path realizations, while the SNR loop wraps around everything since that parameter changes at each level.

content.text:
Đã đọc hết code. Bây giờ có thể thiết kế chính xác:

---

## Cấu trúc file v2_0

```
CSIRS_128T128R_v2_0/
│
├── CSIRS_128T128R_v2_0.m          ← Main file (trục chính, ~80 dòng)
│
├── csirs_buildGrid.m              ← Section 2+3: CSI-RS config + TX grid
├── csirs_channelSetup.m           ← Section 5: CDL channel object
├── csirs_txRx.m                   ← Section 6+7: TX→Channel→AWGN→Demod
├── csirs_channelEstimate.m        ← Section 8: CE per resource → H_est_full
├── csirs_feedback.m               ← Section 10: PMI/RI/CQI (A/B/C/D)
├── csirs_computeMetrics.m         ← MCS mapping + Throughput từ kết quả feedback
└── csirs_printSweepTable.m        ← Format + in bảng kết quả SNR sweep
```

---

## Main file (trục chính)

```matlab
% CSIRS_128T128R_v2_0.m
% SNR Sweep: RI/MCS/Throughput trung bình tại 3-4 mức SNR

clear; close all; clc;

%% ── SECTION 1: SYSTEM CONFIG (cố định, ngoài vòng lặp) ──────────────────
carrier = nrCarrierConfig;
carrier.NSizeGrid         = 52;
carrier.SubcarrierSpacing = 30;

nTxAntennas = 128;  nRxAntennas = 4;
gnbArraySize = [4, 16, 2, 1, 1];
ueArraySize  = [1,  2, 2, 1, 1];

channelCfg.DelayProfile        = 'CDL-B';
channelCfg.DelaySpread         = 450e-9;
channelCfg.MaximumDopplerShift = 136;
channelCfg.CarrierFrequency    = 4.9e9;

%% ── SECTION 2+3: BUILD TX GRID (1 lần, dùng lại cho mọi realization) ────
[csirs, txGrid_2slots, allCsirsInd, allCsirsSym, slotAssign, cdmLengths] = ...
    csirs_buildGrid(carrier, nTxAntennas);

%% ── SECTION 5: CHANNEL OBJECT (tạo 1 lần, release() per realization) ────
channel = csirs_channelSetup(carrier, gnbArraySize, ueArraySize, channelCfg);

%% ── SNR SWEEP ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
snrList  = [-5, 5, 15, 25];    % dB: xa / trung bình / gần / rất gần
nRealiz  = 10;                  % số channel realization để lấy trung bình
results  = [];                  % sẽ là struct(nSNR, 1) sau vòng lặp

for iSNR = 1:length(snrList)
    SNRdB = snrList(iSNR);
    accum = struct('ri',[],'mcs',[],'tp',[]); % [A B C D] x nRealiz

    for iReal = 1:nRealiz
        release(channel);   % reset channel state → new realization

        % Section 6+7: TX→channel→AWGN→demod
        [rxGrid_s0, rxGrid_s1, pathGains, sampleTimes, offset, ofdmInfo] = ...
            csirs_txRx(carrier, txGrid_2slots, allCsirsInd, allCsirsSym, ...
                       channel, SNRdB, nRxAntennas);

        % Section 8: channel estimation
        [H_est_full, nVar_all] = ...
            csirs_channelEstimate(carrier, rxGrid_s0, rxGrid_s1, ...
                                  allCsirsInd, allCsirsSym, slotAssign, ...
                                  cdmLengths, nTxAntennas, nRxAntennas);

        % Section 10: CSI feedback (A/B/C/D)
        fb = csirs_feedback(carrier, csirs, H_est_full, nVar_all, slotAssign);

        % RI + MCS + Throughput từ feedback
        m  = csirs_computeMetrics(fb, carrier);

        accum.ri  = [accum.ri;  m.ri];    % [nRealiz x 4]
        accum.mcs = [accum.mcs; m.mcs];
        accum.tp  = [accum.tp;  m.tp];
    end

    % Trung bình qua nRealiz
    results(iSNR).snr = SNRdB;
    results(iSNR).ri  = mean(accum.ri,  1);   % [1 x 4]: A B C D
    results(iSNR).mcs = mean(accum.mcs, 1);
    results(iSNR).tp  = mean(accum.tp,  1);

    fprintf('SNR=%+3ddB done (%d realizations)\n', SNRdB, nRealiz);
end

%% ── OUTPUT TABLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
csirs_printSweepTable(results, snrList);
```

---

## Phân công từng function file

### `csirs_buildGrid.m`
- **Input:** `carrier`, `nTxAntennas`
- **Output:** `csirs`, `txGrid_2slots`, `allCsirsInd`, `allCsirsSym`, `slotAssign`, `cdmLengths`
- **Nguồn:** Section 2 + 3 hiện tại (giữ nguyên logic)

### `csirs_channelSetup.m`
- **Input:** `carrier`, `gnbArraySize`, `ueArraySize`, `channelCfg`
- **Output:** `channel` (nrCDLChannel object)
- **Nguồn:** Section 5

### `csirs_txRx.m`
- **Input:** `carrier`, `txGrid_2slots`, `allCsirsInd`, `allCsirsSym`, `channel`, `SNRdB`, `nRxAntennas`
- **Output:** `rxGrid_s0`, `rxGrid_s1`, `pathGains`, `sampleTimes`, `offset`, `ofdmInfo`
- **Nguồn:** Section 6 + 7

### `csirs_channelEstimate.m`
- **Input:** `carrier`, `rxGrid_s0`, `rxGrid_s1`, `allCsirsInd`, `allCsirsSym`, `slotAssign`, `cdmLengths`, `nTxAntennas`, `nRxAntennas`
- **Output:** `H_est_full [K×28×nRx×128]`, `nVar_all [1×4]`
- **Nguồn:** Section 8

### `csirs_feedback.m`
- **Input:** `carrier`, `csirs`, `H_est_full`, `nVar_all`, `slotAssign`
- **Output:** `fb` struct: `fb.ri_A/B/C/D`, `fb.cap_A/B/C/D`, `fb.cqi_A/B/C/D`, `fb.W_B/C/D`
- **Nguồn:** Section 10 (toàn bộ logic A/B/C/D)

### `csirs_computeMetrics.m`
- **Input:** `fb` (output của feedback), `carrier`
- **Output:** `m.ri [1×4]`, `m.mcs [1×4]`, `m.tp [1×4]` (Mbps)
- **Logic mới:** CQI→MCS table (TS 38.214), tính throughput = `RI × ModOrd × CodeRate × nRE_per_slot`

### `csirs_printSweepTable.m`
- **Input:** `results` struct, `snrList`
- **Output:** Print bảng so sánh, cột `% vs B`

---

## Điều quan trọng về vòng lặp

```
txGrid_2slots → NGOÀI loop  (CSI-RS symbols không đổi)
channel object → NGOÀI loop  (chỉ gọi release() bên trong)
SNRdB         → trong SNR loop
channel realization → trong Monte Carlo loop (release() → new random path)
```

Section 4 (visualization grid), Section 9 (CE NMSE analysis), Section 11 (beamforming plot) — **bỏ qua trong v2** (chỉ giữ ở v1 để debug), hoặc có thể gọi lại từ main nếu muốn xem một realization cụ thể.

---

Bạn muốn tôi bắt đầu implement file nào trước?
Theo tôi để tiết kiệm công sức, có thể bỏ qua Approach A, vì nó lỗi thời rồi, giờ đã có chuẩn, nên chắc chẳng ai làm giống nó nữa.

%% diag_r3.m — Kiểm chứng nguyên nhân R3 NMSE xấu
%
%  Chạy SAU khi file chính CSIRS_128T128R_v1_0.m đã chạy xong.
%  (KHÔNG clear workspace giữa 2 lần chạy)

---
function [CSIReport, CSIInfo] = myCSIReport(carrier, csirs, reportConfig, dmrsConfig, H, nVar)
%myCSIReport  CSI report (RI/PMI/CQI) via nr5g internal functions.
%   Replicates nrCSIReportCSIRS logic without the matlab.internal.feature gate.
%   Requires nr5g.internal.* on the MATLAB path (local 5g/ copy).

---
%% schemeB_full128.m — Full 128-Port Type-I DFT Codebook (Scheme-B concept)
%
%  Implements codebook-constrained RI/PMI/CQI selection for the full
%  128-port channel using a DFT beam grid, following the Scheme-B
%  concept from R1-2500098 (Huawei, RAN1#120, Feb 2025).
---
% Kiểm tra rank thực tế của H_res từng resource (không qua codebook)
fprintf('Per-resource singular values:\n');
for resIdx = 1:nResources
    portStart = (resIdx-1)*nPortsPerRes + 1;
    portEnd   = resIdx*nPortsPerRes;
---
%% Verification script: Mode B Refined Type I Codebook (TS 38.214 §5.2.2.2.1a)
%  Tests mathematical properties and spec conformance
clear; clc;

addpath(genpath(fullfile(pwd,'5g')));

content.thinking:
Now I have a good picture. Let me analyze each file:

1. `CSIRS_128T128R_v1_0.m` - Main file, keep
2. `diag_r3.m` - Debug script for R3 NMSE issue, runs AFTER main file (workspace dependent). This was a debugging tool. Likely no longer needed since the main code is stable.
3. `myCSIReport.m` - Function that tries to replicate nrCSIReportCSIRS. This was likely an early attempt before using `nr5g.internal.nrPMIReport`. The main file now uses `nr5g.internal.*` directly. Likely no longer needed.


4. `schemeB_full128.m` - Scheme-B concept from R1-2500098, DFT codebook for full 128-port. This is related to Approach B/C development and could serve as a reference, though it's not called by the main file.
5. `testRI.m` and `test_ModeB_verification.m` - Both are debug and verification scripts from development that aren't actively used anymore.
6. The PDF is a 3GPP meeting reference document.

Looking at the remaining files, I see several reference documents including a Huawei contribution and TS 38.214 specification extracts, plus some project documentation and reports in Vietnamese covering the Mode B implementation and Rel-19 upgrade work.

Now I'm categorizing what can be safely removed versus what should stay. The debug and verification scripts like `diag_r3.m`, `testRI.m`, and `test_ModeB_verification.m` are clearly obsolete since they were workspace-dependent and their issues are resolved. The `myCSIReport.m` file is also redundant since it's been replaced by direct calls to the internal function. The `schemeB_full128.m` script is trickier—it implements a DFT codebook approach, but the main implementation uses SVD instead, so it's not actually being called and could be archived or removed. I'll keep the markdown files and readme since they provide project context and documentation.

For the reports and reference materials, those should stay as they document the implementation and provide the technical specifications we're working from. The main code file `CSIRS_128T128R_v1_0.m` is obviously essential. So my recommendation is to delete the four clearly obsolete scripts, ask about `schemeB_full128.m` since it's borderline, and preserve everything else.

```
csirs_channelSetup.m         ← Section 5 từ v1
csirs_txRx.m                 ← Section 6+7 từ v1
csirs_channelEstimate.m      ← Section 8 từ v1

csirs_computeMetrics.m       ← mới: CQI→MCS + Throughput
csirs_printSweepTable.m      ← mới: format bảng kết quả
```

**Cấu hình mặc định trong v2:**
- SNR sweep: `[-5, 10, 25]` dB (xa / trung bình / gần)
- `nRealiz = 20` realization per SNR point
- Channel: CDL-B, 450ns, 136Hz, 4.9GHz (đã apply từ trước)

**Bước tiếp theo:** Chạy `CSIRS_128T128R_v2_0.m` trong MATLAB, xem có lỗi gì không. Lỗi phổ biến nhất có thể là `release(channel)` trên System object — nếu có, báo tôi sửa ngay.
Tôi chưa hiểu, tại sao release(channel) có thể thay thế monte carlo? ý nghĩa hoạt động?

content.thinking: The user is asking about why `release(channel)` can serve as Monte Carlo. Let me explain this clearly.

content.text:
Câu hỏi hay. Thực ra `release(channel)` **không phải** thay thế Monte Carlo — nó là **cơ chế để tạo ra mỗi realization mới** bên trong vòng lặp Monte Carlo. Giải thích:

---

## `nrCDLChannel` là Stateful System Object

MATLAB CDL channel là một **System object** có trạng thái nội bộ:

```
channel(txWaveform)  →  lần 1: dùng random seed nội bộ #1
channel(txWaveform)  →  lần 2: tiếp tục từ trạng thái trước (cùng realization, chỉ thêm thời gian)
channel(txWaveform)  →  lần 3: tiếp tục...
```

Đây là thiết kế đúng cho **1 realization dài** (time-continuous fading). Nhưng với Monte Carlo ta cần **nhiều realization độc lập**.

---

## `release()` làm gì?

```matlab
release(channel)
```

→ Reset hoàn toàn trạng thái nội bộ: random number generator, path gains, delay taps... Lần gọi tiếp theo sẽ **khởi tạo lại từ đầu với seed ngẫu nhiên mới** → tạo ra một realization kênh hoàn toàn khác.

---

## Vòng lặp Monte Carlo thực sự

```matlab
for iReal = 1:nRealiz          % ← đây là vòng Monte Carlo
    release(channel);           % reset → new random realization

    [rxGrid_s0, ...] = csirs_txRx(..., channel, SNRdB, ...);
    %   ↑ bên trong: channel(txWaveform) → realization #iReal
    %                rng không fix → noise cũng khác mỗi lần

    [H_est_full, ...] = csirs_channelEstimate(...);
    fb = csirs_feedback(...);
    m  = csirs_computeMetrics(...);

    acc_ri(iReal, :) = m.ri;    % ← thu thập kết quả
    acc_tp(iReal, :) = m.tp;
end

results(iSNR).ri = mean(acc_ri, 1);   % ← trung bình qua 20 realization
```

`release()` chỉ là **nút reset** để bắt đầu một realization mới. Vòng `for iReal` mới là Monte Carlo thực sự.

---

## Tại sao cần nhiều realization?

Một lần chạy CDL channel chỉ cho 1 tập path gains ngẫu nhiên — tương ứng 1 vị trí UE cụ thể với 1 bộ multipath cụ thể. Kết quả RI/MCS/TP có thể may rủi cao hoặc thấp bất thường.

```
iReal=1:  CDL-B realization #1 → RI=4, TP=280 Mbps
iReal=2:  CDL-B realization #2 → RI=2, TP=150 Mbps
iReal=3:  CDL-B realization #3 → RI=3, TP=210 Mbps
...
iReal=20: trung bình → RI=2.8, TP=195 Mbps  ← con số tin cậy
```

**Trung bình qua nhiều realization = kỳ vọng thống kê = kết quả đại diện cho toàn bộ UE ở mức SNR đó**, không phụ thuộc vào may rủi của 1 lần chạy.

The throughput values seem to be calculating correctly, but I'm noticing the MCS column is displaying the average CQI rather than the actual MCS index, which is creating some confusion. For Mode D, the CQI average of 4.0 seems surprisingly low given the RI=3 configuration, possibly because the SINR values from the codebook are coming out lower than expected. Looking at why the throughput comparison is so skewed, SVD has a significant advantage because it always achieves RI=4 with perfect beamforming, while the Rel-19 codebook modes are constrained by their resolution limits. Since throughput scales with RI, modulation order, and code rate, that RI difference compounds heavily. The counterintuitive part is Mode D showing MCS=4 while Mode C reaches MCS=6-7, even though Mode B uses more bits—this likely happens because Mode D's higher RI=3 introduces more inter-layer interference, degrading the per-layer SINR despite having more layers overall.

Working through the math, Mode A actually delivers higher total throughput than Mode B because Mode B trades per-layer quality for more layers, and that quality loss outweighs the throughput gain from the extra layer. I'm noticing something interesting about the RI values—they're showing exact integers like 2.00 and 3.00 across all 20 realizations, which means the rank selection didn't vary at all. ### Vấn đề 2: Kết quả cần phân tích

Kết quả hiện tại:

```
Approach      SNR=-5dB (xa)                SNR=+10dB (tb)               SNR=+25dB (gần)
              RI    MCS   TP(Mbps)  %vsB   RI    MCS   TP(Mbps)  %vsB   RI    MCS   TP(Mbps)  %vsB
B: SVD (ref)  4.00  6.0   144.1    ref    4.00  7.0   163.6     ref    4.00  7.2   168.9     ref
C: Mode A     2.00  5.0    57.3    39.8%  2.00  6.7    78.4    47.9%  2.00  7.0    81.8    48.4%
D: Mode B     2.00  5.0    57.3    39.8%  3.00  4.0    66.3    40.6%  3.00  4.0    66.3    39.3%
```

**Quan sát đáng chú ý:**

**1. RI_B = 4 luôn ổn định** — CDL-B có SV phân bổ đều, SVD luôn chọn full rank. Đúng kỳ vọng.

**2. Mode A (C) luôn RI=2, Mode B (D) RI=2→3** — Rel-19 codebook bị giới hạn bởi quantization, không đạt RI=4 như SVD. Đây là kết quả thực tế, không phải lỗi.

**3. Nghịch lý Mode B: RI cao hơn nhưng TP thấp hơn Mode A** ← điểm quan trọng cần thảo luận:
- Mode B chọn RI=3 nhưng SINR/layer thấp hơn → MCS=4 (thấp)
- Mode A chọn RI=2 nhưng beam tập trung hơn → MCS=6-7 (cao hơn)
- Kết quả: TP_C > TP_D tại SNR=10/25dB

Đây là hiện tượng **rank-rate tradeoff** thực tế — nhiều layer không phải lúc nào cũng tốt hơn nếu per-layer SINR giảm mạnh. Kết quả này phản ánh đặc tính thực của codebook, không phải lỗi simulation.

**4. % vs B khá thấp (~40-48%)** — chủ yếu vì B có RI=4 còn C/D chỉ đạt RI=2-3. Đây là gap của codebook-constrained feedback so với ideal SVD.

---

content.text:
Bạn đúng, tôi phân tích sai. Bằng chứng rõ ràng:

- **v1** (CDL-B, DS=100ns, Doppler=5Hz, SNR=20dB) → Mode A đạt **RI=4**
- **v2** (CDL-B, DS=450ns, Doppler=136Hz) → Mode A chỉ đạt **RI=2**

Nguyên nhân không phải codebook quantization mà là **tham số kênh đã thay đổi**. Có 2 tác động kỹ thuật:

---

### 1. DS=450ns → Channel estimation accuracy giảm

DS tăng 4.5× → impulse response dài hơn → năng lượng kênh trải rộng hơn trên miền tần số. Với 52 PRBs (624 subcarriers), channel biến thiên mạnh hơn nhiều giữa các subcarrier. Khi tính H_wb bằng cách **average toàn bộ frequency**, ma trận wideband bị "làm mờ" → các singular value thấp bị kéo xuống → RI chọn thấp hơn.

### 2. Doppler=136Hz → Temporal coherence ngắn hơn

Với 2-slot observation (2 × 0.5ms = 1ms) và Doppler=136Hz:

```
Coherence time Tc ≈ 1/(4×136) ≈ 1.84ms
Observation window = 1ms ≈ 0.54 × Tc
```

Channel thay đổi đáng kể trong khi đang đo → H_wb tính ra là **average của channel đang biến thiên** → effective rank của H_wb thấp hơn rank thực của channel.

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the signal processing chain from H (channel estimate) to CQI, and then from CQI to MCS. This is a detailed technical question about the 5G NR signal processing chain. Let me explain this carefully.

Let me trace through the actual code to show what the code actually does vs what the standard specifies.

The user's understanding of the chain is:
H → SNR per RE → SINR_eff → BLER → CQI → MCS

Let me explain each step and compare with what the code actually does.

**Step 1: H → SNR per RE**

From H_wb [nRx × nTx], after beamforming with precoder W:


H_eff = H_wb × W gives me the effective channel [nRx × nLayers]. For the ZF receiver approach, I'm computing the pseudo-inverse to get the combining weights, then calculating the signal power for each layer against the interference from other layers and the noise floor.

With SVD-based beamforming, the right singular vectors create orthogonal layers, so there's no cross-layer interference—just the signal power from each singular value divided by the noise variance.

Now I need to map these per-subcarrier SINR values to an effective SINR for CQI selection. The 3GPP standard uses EESM, which applies an exponential mapping with a calibration parameter to compress the SINR distribution into a single representative value.

In the actual implementation, the code simplifies this by taking a straightforward arithmetic mean of the per-RE SINR values instead of the more sophisticated EESM approach. For the wideband approach, I'm computing SINR directly from a single wideband channel without considering per-subcarrier variation.

From the effective SINR, I then map to a BLER estimate and finally select the appropriate CQI index from the 3GPP table. CS (modulation order + code rate)
- Use AWGN BLER curves to find the maximum CQI such that BLER ≤ 10%
- The BLER curve maps (SINR_eff, MCS) → BLER

In the code (simplified approach):
- Uses a lookup table `cqi_tbl = [-6.7,-4.7,...,22.7]` which are SINR thresholds
- `cqi = max(sum(SINR_dB >= cqi_tbl), 1)` - simply counts how many thresholds the SINR exceeds
- This is a direct SINR-to-CQI mapping WITHOUT going through BLER

The `cqi_tbl` values are essentially pre-computed SINR thresholds for 10% BLER on AWGN channel for each CQI level, which is a valid simplified approach since the BLER calculation gets absorbed into the threshold lookup.

Now for the CQI to MCS conversion: CQI (1-15) maps to MCS through the 3GPP standard tables, where CQI specifies modulation and target code rate while MCS provides a more granular specification with actual code rate. The current implementation treats CQI index as MCS index directly, which is a simplification since the mapping isn't one-to-one.

Looking at the actual code in `csirs_feedback.m`, I see it's using a lookup table that maps SINR directly to CQI by comparing against threshold values.

Then in `csirs_computeMetrics.m`, the code treats the CQI index as if it were an MCS index into the combined table, averaging the CQI values across layers and using that as a direct lookup. This works reasonably well because the CQI and MCS tables happen to have similar structure at corresponding indices, even though technically they're different mappings in the standard.

content.text:
Câu hỏi rất chi tiết. Để trả lời chính xác cần trace qua cả lý thuyết lẫn code thực tế — có điểm code đang làm khác chuẩn. Phân tích từng bước:

---

## Chain đầy đủ theo chuẩn 3GPP

```
H_est → Beamforming → SINR per RE → SINR_eff (MIESM) → BLER lookup → CQI → MCS
```

---

### Bước 1: H → SINR per RE

Sau khi có precoder W từ codebook:

```
H_eff = H_wb × W        [nRx × nLayers]
W_zf  = pinv(H_eff)     [nLayers × nRx]  (ZF combiner)

SINR_layer_l = |W_zf(l,:) × H_eff(:,l)|²
               ─────────────────────────────────────────────
               Σ_{k≠l}|W_zf(l,:)×H_eff(:,k)|² + nVar×||W_zf(l,:)||²
                   ↑ inter-layer interference        ↑ noise
```

Với SVD (Approach B), W = right singular vectors → H×W = U×Σ → các layer **trực giao**, không có inter-layer interference → SINR_l = σ_l²/nVar (đơn giản hơn nhiều).

Trong thực tế phải tính **per RE** (per subcarrier), vì H thay đổi theo tần số do multipath.

---

### Bước 2: SINR per RE → SINR_eff

Đây là bước **quan trọng nhất** — không thể dùng arithmetic mean. Chuẩn 3GPP dùng **MIESM (Mutual Information Effective SINR Mapping)**:

```
MI_k = log2(1 + SINR_k)          ← mutual information từng RE (simplified)

SINR_eff = 2^( (1/N) × Σ_k MI_k ) - 1
```

Hoặc dùng **EESM**:
```
SINR_eff = -β × ln( (1/N) × Σ_k exp(-SINR_k / β) )
```

β phụ thuộc vào modulation order. Mục đích: ánh xạ N giá trị SINR rải rác thành 1 giá trị tương đương **trên kênh AWGN** để tra bảng BLER.

---

### Bước 3: SINR_eff → BLER → CQI

Với SINR_eff đã ra:
- Với mỗi CQI candidate (1→15), tra bảng BLER curve (AWGN, MCS tương ứng)
- Chọn **CQI lớn nhất** thỏa mãn BLER ≤ 10%

BLER curve là bảng pre-computed (hoặc look-up table SINR_threshold per CQI):

```
CQI  Modulation  Code Rate  SINR threshold (10% BLER)
 1   QPSK        78/1024       -6.7 dB
 3   QPSK        449/1024      -2.3 dB
 7   16QAM       466/1024       6.9 dB
15   256QAM      948/1024      22.7 dB
```

---

### Bước 4: CQI → MCS

CQI (15 giá trị) → MCS (28 giá trị trong TS 38.214 Table 5.1.3.1-1) thông qua mapping. CQI thô hơn MCS, nhưng cùng xác định `{ModOrder, CodeRate}`. Thường:

```
CQI  → {ModOrder, CodeRate} → tra MCS table → MCS index gần nhất
```

---

## Code hiện tại đang làm gì (và sai ở đâu)

### Approach B — đúng cơ bản
```matlab
% SINR per layer tính đúng theo ZF
sinr_B_dB = 10*log10(sinr_B);
% Map SINR → CQI bằng threshold lookup (thay cho BLER curve đầy đủ) — đủ dùng
cqi_B = arrayfun(@(s) max(sum(s >= cqi_tbl), 1), sinr_B_dB);
```
Đây là **wideband SINR** (1 giá trị per layer), bỏ qua per-RE variation.

### Approach C/D — đơn giản hoá
```matlab
% nrPMIReport trả về SINRPerREPMI: [nRE × nLayers]
sinr_C_dB = 10*log10(mean(info_C.SINRPerREPMI, 1, 'omitnan'));
%                         ↑ arithmetic mean — SAI so với MIESM
```

Dùng **arithmetic mean** thay vì MIESM → overestimate SINR_eff trên kênh fading (vì 1 RE xấu kéo throughput xuống nhiều hơn 1 RE tốt kéo lên).

### csirs_computeMetrics — đang dùng CQI index = MCS index
```matlab
mcsIdx = round(mean(cqiVec));  % ← gần đúng nhưng không chính xác
```
CQI index và MCS index không phải cùng một bảng. CQI 7 ≠ MCS 7 về mặt spec.

---

## Tóm tắt mức độ ảnh hưởng

| Bước | Code hiện tại | Chuẩn đầy đủ | Sai số |
|---|---|---|---|
| H → SINR/layer | ZF, wideband | ZF, per-RE | Trung bình → chấp nhận được |
| SINR aggregation | Arithmetic mean | MIESM/EESM | Overestimate nhẹ |
| SINR → CQI | Threshold table | BLER curve lookup | Tương đương (threshold = pre-computed 10% BLER) |
| CQI → MCS | CQI index ≈ MCS index | Proper table mapping | Sai nhỏ ở một số điểm |

Với mục đích **so sánh tương đối** giữa B/C/D, các sai số này **nhất quán** giữa các approach nên không ảnh hưởng kết luận. Chỉ cần cẩn thận khi báo cáo giá trị tuyệt đối (TP Mbps) cho khách.
Trong workspace có @5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/nrCQISelect.m hình như có thể tính CQI @5g/netmod/+nr5g/+internal/computeMCS.m  có thể tính MCS, có phải nó sẽ đúng chuẩn hơn không?

Now I can see the full picture:

1. `nrCQISelect` uses `nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect` which is the full Link-to-System Mapping (L2SM) implementation
2. L2SM uses MIESM (Mutual Information Effective SINR Mapping) - this is the proper 3GPP-compliant approach
3. `computeMCS` also uses `nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect` with a MCS table instead of CQI table

The key insight:
- Both functions use `nr5g.internal.L2SM` which is the proper MIESM implementation


- `nrCQISelect` outputs CQI values (1-15) with a 10% BLER threshold while `computeMCS` outputs MCS values (0-27) using the full MCS table, both of which would be more accurate than our current arithmetic mean approach with direct threshold mapping
- There's a compatibility constraint though: `nrCQISelect` expects a struct-based reportConfig with Type1SinglePanel codebook, but our approach uses different parameters like `SINRPerREPMI` from `nrPMIReport` (Rel-19 style)
- For approaches C and D, I can feed the `SINRPerREPMI` directly to an internal `cqiSelect` function, while approach B would compute SINR per layer from SVD and pass it to L2SM, but I need to figure out how to adapt `nrCQISelect` for the Rel-19 codebook since it doesn't natively support `typeI-SinglePanel-r19`

Looking at the available functions, `computeMCS` takes per-RE SINR values and handles the MCS computation using L2SM, which is more flexible than `nrCQISelect` for our needs. However, it requires an `l2smSRS` object and `pdsch` config that would add unnecessary complexity to our implementation.

The most practical path forward is to stick with `nrPMIReport` for PMI and RI selection in approaches C and D, then pass the resulting `SINRPerREPMI` through to the CQI selection step. nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect` for proper CQI
3. For MCS, use `computeMCS` or the L2SM approach

But for now, let me just answer the user's question: YES, these functions would give more accurate results, especially because they use L2SM (MIESM-based) rather than arithmetic mean. The `nrCQISelect` is particularly useful for Approach C/D since we could use it to get proper CQI from the SINR values returned by nrPMIReport.

Actually, looking more carefully:
- `nrCQISelect` takes the full H and does PMI+CQI together. For Rel-19 codebook, it might not support it.
- But the internal `cqiSelect` function takes SINRs directly.

The key question is: can we use the internal L2SM function directly with the SINRPerREPMI from nrPMIReport? That would be the most accurate path. Checking the documentation, `nrCQISelect` doesn't support the Rel-19 codebook type, so for Approach C/D I'd need to stick with `nrPMIReport` for PMI and RI selection, then feed those SINR values into the internal L2SM function for CQI computation.

The improvement would be: keep the current PMI/RI selection, but replace the arithmetic mean SINR to CQI lookup with the L2SM-based `cqiSelect` function that accounts for BLER properly, then use `computeMCS` to get the final MCS index.

content.text:
Câu trả lời: **Có, đúng hơn đáng kể.** Đây là lý do:

---

## `nrCQISelect` dùng gì bên trong?

Trace từ code vừa đọc:

```
nrCQISelect(...)
  └─ cqiSelect(l2sm, carrier, pdsch, overhead, SINRPerREPMI, cqiTable)
       └─ nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect(...)   ← L2SM = Link-to-System Mapping
            └─ MIESM: Mutual Information Effective SINR Mapping
                 → effectiveSINR (đúng chuẩn)
                 → transportBLER (thực sự tính BLER!)
                 → cqiIndex (CQI thỏa BLER ≤ 10%)
```

`computeMCS` cũng dùng cùng `L2SM.cqiSelect` nhưng với MCS table thay vì CQI table → ra MCS index (0–27) trực tiếp.

---

## So sánh với code hiện tại

| Bước | Code hiện tại | `nrCQISelect` / `computeMCS` |
|---|---|---|
| SINR aggregation | `mean(SINRPerREPMI)` — arithmetic mean | **MIESM** — mutual information weighted |
| BLER | Không tính, dùng threshold table | **Tính thực sự** → chọn CQI thỏa BLER ≤ 10% |
| CQI → MCS | CQI index ≈ MCS index (sai) | **L2SM + MCS table** → MCS index đúng chuẩn |

---

## Vấn đề khi áp dụng cho C/D (Rel-19)

```matlab
% nrCQISelect docstring:
% CodebookType must be one of:
%   {'Type1SinglePanel', 'Type1MultiPanel', 'Type2', 'eType2'}
% ← KHÔNG có 'typeI-SinglePanel-r19'
```

`nrCQISelect` **không hỗ trợ Rel-19 codebook** → không thể gọi trực tiếp cho Approach C/D.

**Giải pháp:** Giữ `nrPMIReport` để chọn PMI/RI (Rel-19), sau đó lấy `SINRPerREPMI` nó trả về, rồi feed thẳng vào L2SM:

```matlab
% Approach C/D (hiện tại):
[~, info_C] = nr5g.internal.nrPMIReport(carrier, csirs{1}, repCfg, ri_C, H_4d, nVar_wb);
sinr_C_dB = 10*log10(mean(info_C.SINRPerREPMI, 1));  % ← arithmetic mean, sai

% Cải thiện: feed SINRPerREPMI vào L2SM trực tiếp
l2sm = nr5g.internal.L2SM.initialize(carrier);
[l2sm, cqi_C, sinr_eff_C, bler_C] = nr5g.internal.L2SM.cqiSelect(...
    l2sm, carrier, pdsch, 0, ...
    10*log10(info_C.SINRPerREPMI), ...   % per-RE SINR in dB
    cqiTable, 0.1);
```

Với `computeMCS` cho MCS trực tiếp:
```matlab
mcs_C = nr5g.internal.computeMCS(l2smSRS, carrier, pdsch, info_C.SINRPerREPMI, 'qam64');
%                                                                 ↑ linear SINR per RE
```

---

## Kết luận: Có nên thay không?

**Nên**, nhưng có trade-off:

| | Cải thiện | Phức tạp thêm |
|---|---|---|
| **L2SM cho CQI** | MIESM thay arithmetic mean → chính xác hơn ~1-2 CQI | Cần init `l2sm`, cần `pdsch` config |
| **computeMCS** | MCS index đúng chuẩn (0-27) | Cần `l2smSRS` object (khác `l2sm`) |

Với mục tiêu **so sánh tương đối B/C/D**, cải thiện CQI bằng L2SM là đáng làm. Còn `computeMCS` thêm phức tạp mà gain nhỏ hơn — CQI đã đủ để so sánh, MCS chỉ cần khi muốn báo cáo MCS index chính xác tuyệt đối.

Bạn muốn tôi implement cải thiện MIESM-based CQI vào `csirs_feedback.m` không?

"nrCQISelect không hỗ trợ Rel-19 codebook → không thể gọi trực tiếp cho Approach C/D." có thể sửa nrCQISelect vì dự án tương lai vẫn dùng cần dùng. "Còn computeMCS thêm phức tạp mà gain nhỏ hơn — CQI đã đủ để so sánh, MCS chỉ cần khi muốn báo cáo MCS index chính xác tuyệt đối." có, tôi muốn tính MCS chính xác và đúng chuẩn, tương lai dự án khác cao cấp hơn cần dùng.

                % CQI mode, as the SINRPerSubband field in the PMI information
                % output has the SINR values according to the PMIMode
                if strcmpi(reportConfig.PMIMode,'Wideband')
                    % If PMI mode is 'Wideband', only one i2 value is reported
                    % and the SINR values are obtained for the entire BWP in
                    % the SINRPerSubband field of PMIInfo output. In this case
                    % compute the SINR values corresponding to subband or
                    % wideband based on the CQI mode
                    SINRperSubband = getSubbandSINR(sinrPerREPMI,CQISubbandInfo,csirsIndSubs_k);
                else
                    % If PMI mode is 'Subband', when codebook type is specified
                    % as 'Type1SinglePanel', one i2 value is reported per
                    % subband and when codebook type is specified as
                    % 'Type1MultiPanel', a set of three indices [i20; i21; i22]
                    % are reported per subband. The SINR values are obtained in
                    % subband level granularity from PMI selection function.
                    % Extract the SINR values accordingly
                    for subbandIdx = 1:size(PMISet.i2,2)
                        if ~any(isnan(PMISet.i2(:,subbandIdx)))
                            if strcmpi(reportConfig.CodebookType,'Type1MultiPanel')
                                SINRperSubband(subbandIdx,:) = PMIInfo.SINRPerSubband(subbandIdx,:,PMISet.i2(1,subbandIdx),PMISet.i2(2,subbandIdx),PMISet.i2(3,subbandIdx),PMISet.i1(1),PMISet.i1(2),PMISet.i1(3),PMISet.i1(4),PMISet.i1(5),PMISet.i1(6));
                            else
                                SINRperSubband(subbandIdx,:) = PMIInfo.SINRPerSubband(subbandIdx,:,PMISet.i2(subbandIdx),PMISet.i1(1),PMISet.i1(2),PMISet.i1(3));
                            end
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        % Get SINR per subband
        SINRperSubbandperCW = zeros(CQISubbandInfo.NumSubbands,numCodewords);
        for subbandIdx = 1:CQISubbandInfo.NumSubbands
            % Get the SINR values per layer and calculate the SINR values
            % corresponding to each codeword
            layerSINRs = squeeze(SINRperSubband(subbandIdx,:));

            if ~any(isnan(layerSINRs))
                codewordSINRs = cellfun(@sum,nrLayerDemap(layerSINRs));
            else
                % If the linear SINR values of the codeword are NaNs, which
                % implies, there are no CSI-RS resources in the current
                % subband. So, the SINR values for the codewords are
                % considered as NaNs for the particular subband
                codewordSINRs = NaN(1,numCodewords);
            end
            SINRperSubbandperCW(subbandIdx,:) = codewordSINRs;
        end

        if size(SINRperSubbandperCW,1) > 1
            % Compute the wideband SINR value as a mean of the subband SINRs,
            % if either CQI or PMI are configured in subband mode
            SINRperSubbandperCW = [mean(SINRperSubbandperCW,1,'omitnan'); SINRperSubbandperCW];
        end
    end

    BLERForAllSubbands = zeros(CQISubbandInfo.NumSubbands,numCodewords);

    %Initialize L2SM for CQISelection calculation
    l2sm = nr5g.internal.L2SM.initialize(carrier);

    if ~inputSINRTable
        % Get CSI reference resource for CQI selection, as defined in TS
        % 38.214 Section 5.2.2.5
        [pdsch,pdschExt] = nrCSIReferenceResource(carrier,reportConfig,nLayers);

toolUseResult.originalFile: function [CQI,PMISet,CQIInfo,PMIInfo] = nrCQISelect(carrier,varargin) % nrCQISelect PDSCH Channel quality indicator calculation % [CQI,PMISET,CQIINFO,PMIINFO] = nrCQISelect(CARRIER,CSIRS,REPORTCONFIG,NLAYERS,H) % returns channel quality indicator (CQI) values CQI and precoding matrix % indicator (PMI) values PMISET, as defined in TS 38.214 Section 5.2.2.2, % for the specified carrier configuration CARRIER, CSI-RS configuration % CSIRS, channel state information (CSI) reporting configuration % REPORTCONFIG, number of transmission layers NLAYERS, and estimated % channel information H. The function also returns the additional % information about the signal to interference and noise ratio (SINR) % values that are used for the CQI computation and PMI computation. % % CARRIER is a carrier specific configuration object, as described in % nrCarrierConfig. Only these object properties are relevant for this % function: % % SubcarrierSpacing - Subcarrier spacing in kHz % CyclicPrefix - Cyclic prefix type % NSizeGrid - Number of resource blocks (RBs) in % carrier resource grid % NStartGrid - Start of carrier resource grid relative to common % resource block 0 (CRB 0) % NSlot - Slot number % NFrame - System frame number % % CSI-RS resources, as described in nrCSIRSConfig. Only these object % properties are relevant for this function: % % CSIRSType - Type of a CSI-RS resource {'ZP', 'NZP'} % CSIRSPeriod - CSI-RS slot periodicity and offset % RowNumber - Row number corresponding to a CSI-RS resource, as % defined in TS 38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1 % Density - CSI-RS resource frequency density % SymbolLocations - Time-domain locations of a CSI-RS resource % SubcarrierLocations - Frequency-domain locations of a CSI-RS resource % NumRB - Number of RBs allocated for a CSI-RS resource % RBOffset - Starting RB index of CSI-RS allocation relative % to carrier resource grid % For better results, it is recommended to use the same CSI-RS % resource(s) that are used for channel estimate, because the resource % elements (REs) that does not contain the CSI-RS may have the % interpolated channel estimates. Note that the CDM lengths and the % number of ports configured for all the CSI-RS resources must be same. % % REPORTCONFIG is a CSI reporting configuration structure with these % fields: % NSizeBWP - Size of the bandwidth part (BWP) in terms of number % of physical resource blocks (PRBs). It must be a % scalar and the value must be in the range 1...275. % Empty ([]) is also supported and it implies that the % value of NSizeBWP is equal to the size of carrier % resource grid % NStartBWP - Starting PRB index of BWP relative to common resource % block 0 (CRB 0). It must be a scalar and the value % must be in the range 0...2473. Empty ([]) is also % supported and it implies that the value of NStartBWP % is equal to the start of carrier resource grid % CQITable - Optional. Channel quality indicator table. It must % be one of {'table1', 'table2', 'table3'}, as defined % in TS 38.214 Tables 5.2.2.1-2 through 5.2.2.1-4. The % default value is 'table1' % CodebookType - Optional. The type of codebooks according to which % the CSI parameters must be computed. It must be a % character array or a string scalar. It must be one of % {'Type1SinglePanel', 'Type1MultiPanel', 'Type2', 'eType2'}. % In case of 'Type1SinglePanel', the PMI computation is % performed using TS 38.214 Tables 5.2.2.2.1-1 to % 5.2.2.2.1-12. In case of 'Type1MultiPanel', the PMI % computation is performed using TS 38.214 Tables % 5.2.2.2.2-1 to 5.2.2.2.2-6. In case of 'Type2' the % computation is performed according to TS 38.214 % Section 5.2.2.2.3. In case of 'eType2' the % computation is performed according to TS 38.214 % Section 5.2.2.2.5. The default value is % 'Type1SinglePanel' % PanelDimensions - Antenna panel configuration. % - When CodebookType field is specified as % 'Type1SinglePanel' or 'Type2' or 'eType2', this % field is a two-element vector in the form of [N1 % N2]. N1 represents the number of antenna % elements in horizontal direction and N2 % represents the number of antenna elements in % vertical direction. Valid combinations of % [N1 N2] are defined in TS 38.214 Table 5.2.2.2.1-2. % This field is not applicable when the number of % CSI-RS ports is less than or equal to 2 % - When CodebookType field is specified as % 'Type1MultiPanel', this field is a three element % vector in the form of [Ng N1 N2], where Ng % represents the number of antenna panels. Valid % combinations of [Ng N1 N2] are defined in TS % 38.214 Table 5.2.2.2.2-1 % CQIMode - Optional. It represents the mode of CQI reporting. It % must be a character array or a string scalar. It must % be one of {'Subband', 'Wideband'}. The default value % is 'Wideband' % PMIMode - Optional. It represents the mode of PMI reporting. It % must be a character array or a string scalar. It must % be one of {'Subband', 'Wideband'}. The default value % is 'Wideband' % SubbandSize - Subband size for CQI or PMI reporting, provided by % the higher-layer parameter NSBPRB. It must be a % positive scalar and must be one of two possible % subband sizes, as defined in TS 38.214 Table % 5.2.1.4-2. It is applicable only when either CQIMode % or PMIMode are provided as 'Subband' and the size of % BWP is greater than or equal to 24 PRBs % PRGSize - Optional. Precoding resource block group (PRG) size % for CQI calculation, provided by the higher-layer % parameter pdsch-BundleSizeForCSI. This field is % applicable to the CSI report quantity cri-RI-i1-CQI, % as defined in TS 38.214 Section 5.2.1.4.2. This % report quantity expects only the i1 set of PMI to be % reported as part of CSI parameters and PMI mode is % expected to be 'Wideband'. But, for the computation % of the CQI in this report quantity, PMI i2 values are % needed for each PRG. Hence, the PMI output, when this % field is configured, is given as a set of i2 values, % one for each PRG of the specified size. It must be a % scalar and it must be one of {2, 4}. Empty ([]) is % also supported to represent that this field is not % configured by higher layers. If it is present and not % configured as empty, the CQI values are computed % according to the configured CQIMode and the PMI value % is reported for each PRG irrespective of PMIMode. % This field is applicable only when the CodebookType % is configured as 'Type1SinglePanel'. The default % value is [] % CodebookMode - Optional. It represents the codebook mode and it must % be a scalar. The value must be one of {1, 2}. % - When CodebookType is specified as % 'Type1SinglePanel', this field is applicable % only if the number of transmission layers is 1 % or 2 and number of CSI-RS ports is greater than % 2 % - When CodebookType is specified as % 'Type1MultiPanel', this field is applicable for % all the number of transmission layers and the % CodebookMode value 2 is applicable only for the % panel configurations with Ng value 2 % This field is not applicable for CodebookType % 'Type2' or 'eType2'. The default value is 1 % CodebookSubsetRestriction % - Optional. It is a binary vector (right-msb) which % represents the codebook subset restriction. % - When the CodebookType is specified as % 'Type1SinglePanel' or 'Type1MultiPanel' and the % number of CSI-RS ports is greater than 2, the % length of the input vector must be N1*N2*O1*O2, % where N1 and N2 are panel configurations obtained % from PanelDimensions field and O1 and O2 are the % respective discrete Fourier transform (DFT) % oversampling factors obtained from TS.38.214 % Table 5.2.2.2.1-2 for 'Type1SinglePanel' codebook % type or TS.38.214 Table 5.2.2.2.2-1 for % 'Type1MultiPanel' codebook type. When the number % of CSI-RS ports is 2, the applicable codebook % type is 'Type1SinglePanel' and the length of the % input vector must be 6, as defined in TS 38.214 % Section 5.2.2.2.1 % - When CodebookType is specified as 'Type2' or % 'eType2', this field is a bit vector which is % obtained by concatenation of two bit vectors % [B1 B2]. B1 is a bit vector of 11 bits (right-msb) % when N2 of the panel dimensions is greater than % 1 and 0 bits otherwise. B2 is a combination of % 4 bit vectors, each of length 2*N1*N2. B1 % denotes 4 sets of beam groups for which % restriction is applicable. When CodebookType is % specified as 'Type2', B2 denotes the maximum % allowable amplitude for each of the DFT vectors % in each of the respective beam groups denoted by % B1. When CodebookType is specified as 'eType2', % B2 denotes the maximum average coefficient % amplitude for each of the DFT vectors in each of % the respective beam groups denoted by B1. The % default value is empty ([]), which means there % is no codebook subset restriction % i2Restriction - Optional. It is a binary vector which represents the % restricted i2 values in a codebook. Length of the % input vector must be 16. First element of the input % binary vector corresponds to i2 as 0, second element % corresponds to i2 as 1, and so on. Binary value 1 % indicates that the precoding matrix associated with % the respective i2 is unrestricted and 0 indicates % that the precoding matrix associated with the % respective i2 is restricted. For a precoding matrices % codebook, if the number of possible i2 values are % less than 16, then only the required binary elements % are considered and the trailing extra elements in the % input vector are ignored. This field is applicable % only when the number of CSI-RS ports is greater than % 2 and the CodebookType field is specified as % 'Type1SinglePanel'. This field is not applicable when % CodebookType is specified as 'Type2'. The default % value is empty ([]), which means there is no i2 % restriction % NumberOfBeams - It is a scalar which represents the number of beams % to be considered in the beam group. This field is % applicable only when the CodebookType is specified as % 'Type2'. The value must be one of {2, 3, 4} % PhaseAlphabetSize % - Optional. It is a scalar which represents the range % of the phases that are to be considered for the % computation of PMI i2 indices. This field is % applicable only when the CodebookType is specified as % 'Type2'. The value must be one of {4, 8}. The value 4 % represents the phases corresponding to QPSK and the % value 8 represents the phases corresponding to 8-PSK. % The default value is 4. This field is not a % configurable parameter for 'eType2' and it is fixed % as 16, which corresponds to 16-PSK % SubbandAmplitude % - Optional. It is a logical scalar which enables the % reporting of amplitudes per subband when set to true % and disables subband amplitude in PMI reporting when % set to false. The value must be one of {true, false}. % This field is applicable when CodebookType is % specified as 'Type2' and PMIMode is 'Subband'. The % default value is false % ParameterCombination % - Optional. It is a positive scalar integer in the % range 1...8. This field is applicable when % CodebookType is specified as 'eType2'. This % parameter defines the number of beams and two other % parameters as defined in TS 38.214 Table 5.2.2.2.5-1. % The default value is 1 % NumberOfPMISubbandsPerCQISubband % - Optional. It is a positive scalar integer and it must % be either 1 or 2. This field is applicable when % CodebookType is specified as 'eType2'. It represents % the number of PMI subbands within one CQI subband. % The default value is 1 % % The detailed explanation of the CodebookSubsetRestriction field is % present in nr5g.internal.nrDLPMISelect function. % % NLAYERS is a scalar representing the number of transmission layers. % When CodebookType is specified as 'Type1SinglePanel', its value must be % in the range of 1...8. When CodebookType is specified as % 'Type1MultiPanel', its value must be in the range of 1...4. When % CodebookType is specified as 'Type2', its value must be in the range of % 1...2. When CodebookType is specified as 'eType2', its value must be in % the range of 1...4. % % H is the channel estimation matrix. It is of size % K-by-L-by-nRxAnts-by-Pcsirs, where K is the number of subcarriers in % the carrier resource grid, L is the number of orthogonal frequency % division multiplexing (OFDM) symbols spanning one slot, nRxAnts is the % number of receive antennas, and Pcsirs is the number of CSI-RS antenna % ports. Note that the number of transmission layers provided must be % less than or equal to min(nRxAnts,Pcsirs). % % CQI output is a 2-dimensional matrix of size 1-by-numCodewords when CQI % reporting mode is 'Wideband' and (numSubbands+1)-by-numCodewords when % CQI reporting mode is 'Subband'. numSubbands is the number of subbands % and numCodewords is the number of codewords. The first row consists of % 'Wideband' CQI value and if the CQI mode is 'Subband', the 'Wideband' % CQI value is followed by the subband differential CQI values for each % subband. The subband differential values are scalars ranging from 0 to % 3 and these values are computed based on the offset level, as defined % in TS 38.214 Table 5.2.2.1-1, where % subband CQI offset level = subband CQI index - wideband CQI index. % % Note that when the PRGSize field in the reportConfig is configured as % other than empty, it is assumed that the report quantity as reported by % the higher layers is 'cri-RI-i1-CQI'. In this case the SINR values for % the CQI computation are chosen based on the i1 values reported in % PMISet and a valid random i2 value from all the reported i2 values in % the PMISet. In this case, i2 values reported in the PMISet correspond % to each PRG. When CQI reporting mode is 'Wideband', one i2 value is % chosen randomly, for the entire BWP, from the set of i2 values of all % PRGs. When CQI reporting mode is subband, one i2 value is chosen % randomly, for each subband, from the set of PRGs that span the % particular subband. Considering this set of i2 values for indexing, the % corresponding SINR values are used for CQI computation. % % PMISET output is a structure representing the set of PMI indices % (1-based). The detailed explanation of PMISET is available in the % nrDLPMISelect function. % % CQIINFO is an output structure for the CQI information with these % fields: % SINRPerSubbandPerCW - It represents the linear SINR values in each % subband for all the codewords. It is a % two-dimensional matrix of size % - 1-by-numCodewords, when CQI reporting mode % is 'Wideband' % - (numSubbands + 1)-by-numCodewords, when % CQI reporting mode is 'Subband' % Each column of the matrix contains wideband SINR % value (the average SINR value across all % subbands) followed by the SINR values of each % subband. The SINR value in each subband is taken % as an average of SINR values of all the REs % across the particular subband spanning one slot % SINRPerRBPerCW - It represents the linear SINR values in each % RB for all the codewords. It is a % three-dimensional matrix of size % NSizeBWP-by-L-by-numCodewords. The SINR value in % each RB is taken as an average of SINR values of % all the REs across the RB spanning one slot % SubbandCQI - It represents the subband CQI values. It is a % two-dimensional matrix of size % - 1-by-numCodewords, when CQI reporting mode % is 'Wideband' % - (numSubbands + 1)-by-numCodewords, when % CQI reporting mode is 'Subband' % Each column of the matrix contains the absolute % CQI value of wideband followed by the absolute % CQI values corresponding to each subband % TransportBLER - Estimated transport block error rate (BLER) for % each element in SubbandCQI % % Note that the CQI output and all the fields of CQIINFO are returned as % NaNs for these cases: % - When CSI-RS is not present in the operating slot or in the BWP % - When the reported PMISet is all NaNs % Also note that the subband differential CQI value or SubbandCQI value % is reported as NaNs in the subbands where CSI-RS is not present. % % PMIINFO is an output structure with the information about SINR values, % codebook, and the precoding matrix. The detailed explanation for % PMIINFO is given under INFO output in the nrDLPMISelect function. % % [CQI,PMISET,CQIINFO,PMIINFO] = nrCQISelect(CARRIER,CSIRS,REPORTCONFIG,NLAYERS,H,NVAR) % specifies the estimated noise variance at the receiver NVAR as a % nonnegative scalar. This syntax only supports the SISO case. % % BWP is a structure with these fields: % NStartBWP - The starting PRB index of the BWP relative to the % CRB 0 % NSizeBWP - The size of BWP in terms of number of PRBs % % CQICONFIG is a structure of configuration parameters required for CQI % reporting with these fields: % CQIMode - Optional. It represents the mode of CQI reporting. It % must be a character array or a string scalar. It must % be one of {'Subband', 'Wideband'}. The default value % is 'Wideband' % NSBPRB - Subband size, as provided by the higher layers, is % one of two possible subband sizes according to TS % 38.214 Table 5.2.1.4-2. This field is applicable only % when CQIMode is 'Subband' % SINR90pc - Optional. Vector of 15 SINR values in dB. Each value % corresponds to a CQI value at which the BLER must be a % maximum of 0.1.
